domain: earth systems / hydrogeology (karst aquifer conduit network)
specific_mechanism: Karst aquifers route groundwater along preferential conduits dissolved in carbonate bedrock; flow is heterogeneous (turbulent conduits + slow matrix), strongly history-dependent (conduits enlarge with throughput), and gated at epikarst recharge boundaries; storm response is bimodal (fast conduit flow + slow matrix baseflow)
llm_application: Context retrieval as karst-style routing: most queries take a slow 'matrix' path through embedding similarity; but high-throughput queries carve dedicated 'conduits' (cached retrieval pathways that enlarge with reuse and gate at epikarst-style entry filters). Bimodal retrieval response time is the diagnostic. Conduits self-prune via the agent's analog of dissolution: high-use paths get faster, low-use paths atrophy back into matrix.
novelty_claim: Existing RAG/cache work uses LRU or semantic-similarity scoring (GPTCache, semantic cache). No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a bimodal conduit+matrix retrieval architecture with throughput-driven conduit enlargement and epikarst-style entry gating.
